codeReview.summary: Clean, minimal implementation that exactly covers all feature requirements. The worker added a .heatmap case to NavigationSection enum in NetMonitorCore with the 'chart.bar.fill' icon, created a simple HeatmapProjectListView placeholder with the exact empty state text specified ('Import a floor plan to start a Wi-Fi survey'), wired it into ContentView's sectionView(for:), and accessibility ID 'sidebar_heatmap' is automatically set via the existing ForEach pattern in SidebarView. The code is 59 lines across 4 files (including pbxproj regeneration), follows existing conventions perfectly, both targets build, and lint/format pass. No bugs, missing edge cases, or style violations.

issues.description: The .heatmap case is placed first in the enum, making it appear first in CaseIterable.allCases and therefore first in the sidebar COMMAND section (before Tools and Settings). This is likely intentional per the 'top-level sidebar navigation item' requirement, but worth noting as it changes the visual order for users — Heatmap now appears above Tools.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly deviated from Step 2 (TDD) of the macos-ui-worker skill because this feature has no ViewModel or business logic — it's a static placeholder view and an enum case. The worker's suggestion that the skill procedure note TDD is optional for pure navigation/placeholder features is reasonable and would prevent future workers from spending time justifying the same deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0]: 'Skipped ViewModel tests since this feature has no ViewModel — it's a static empty state view and an enum case addition'. The feature literally adds a 42-line static SwiftUI view and a 2-line enum addition.

summary: Feature passes review. All 5 expected behaviors are met: Heatmap appears in sidebar via NavigationSection.allCases, clicking it renders HeatmapProjectListView, empty state shows 'Import a floor plan to start a Wi-Fi survey', accessibility ID sidebar_heatmap is set via the existing SidebarView pattern, and both macOS and iOS targets build. Implementation is minimal and correct — 42-line placeholder view, 2 lines added to NavigationSection enum, 3 lines added to ContentView switch. No blocking issues.
